overview:
The root cause is an incomplete module reorganization: `hide_qt_warning` and `QtWarningFilter` were intended to move from `qutebrowser/utils/log.py` to `qutebrowser/utils/qtlog.py`, but the move was never executed. Both symbols remain exclusively in `log.py` (lines 362–371 and 404–419). As a result, the production caller (`qtnetworkdownloads.py:124`) and the test class `TestHideQtWarning` (`test_log.py:343–369`) still reference the old module, and `test_qtlog.py` has no coverage for these symbols at all.

The fix strategy is a verbatim move in five coordinated edits, ordered so that the new definitions exist before any reference is updated:
1. Add `QtWarningFilter` and `hide_qt_warning` to `qtlog.py` (the destination module).
2. Remove both symbols from `log.py` (the source module).
3. Update the production import in `qtnetworkdownloads.py`.
4. Move `TestHideQtWarning` into `test_qtlog.py` (updating the import reference).
5. Remove `TestHideQtWarning` from `test_log.py`.

All existing imports needed by the moved code (`contextlib`, `logging`, `Iterator`) are already present in `qtlog.py`, so no new import lines are required. The filtering logic itself is correct and must be preserved verbatim — only the module location changes.

edits.change_rationale: req-007 (TO_BE_MISSING) and req-008 (TO_BE_MISSING) both require that `hide_qt_warning` and `QtWarningFilter` be defined here. The must_co_edit_relations state 'QtWarningFilter must be added to qtlog.py at the same time as hide_qt_warning'. The dependency_propagation confirms qtlog.py already imports `contextlib`, `logging`, and `Iterator` (lines 20–26), so no new imports are needed. The similar_implementation_patterns note that `disable_qt_msghandler` (qtlog.py:48–68) uses the identical `@contextlib.contextmanager + try/finally` pattern, which should be followed.
preserved_findings: def hide_qt_warning(pattern: str, logger: str = 'qt') -> Iterator[None]:  # context manager in qutebrowser/utils/qtlog.py that temporarily suppresses Qt log warnings whose message starts with the given pattern for the specified logger
class QtWarningFilter(logging.Filter):  # in qutebrowser/utils/qtlog.py; __init__(self, pattern: str); filter(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> bool
qutebrowser/utils/log.py:362-371 -- reference implementation of hide_qt_warning (to be moved verbatim to qtlog.py)
qutebrowser/utils/log.py:404-419 -- reference implementation of QtWarningFilter (to be moved verbatim to qtlog.py)
qutebrowser/utils/qtlog.py:48-68 -- disable_qt_msghandler uses the same @contextlib.contextmanager + try/finally pattern
qutebrowser/utils/qtlog.py:26 already imports Iterator from typing -- no new import needed
qutebrowser/utils/qtlog.py:20-21 already imports contextlib and logging -- no new imports needed for the move
log.py:365 -- log_filter = QtWarningFilter(pattern) instantiated inside hide_qt_warning
log.py:366 -- logger_obj = logging.getLogger(logger) fetches the named logger
log.py:367 -- logger_obj.addFilter(log_filter) attaches filter for context duration
log.py:371 -- logger_obj.removeFilter(log_filter) cleans up on exit
log.py:414: self._pattern = pattern (stored in __init__)
log.py:418: self._pattern consumed in filter() via record.msg.strip().startswith(self._pattern)
log.py:418 -- do_log = not record.msg.strip().startswith(self._pattern) is the actual suppression predicate
QtWarningFilter must be added alongside hide_qt_warning (req-007) since hide_qt_warning instantiates it at log.py:365

edits.change_rationale: The must_co_edit_relations require: 'If hide_qt_warning is added to qtlog.py -> qutebrowser/utils/log.py:362-371 must be removed or re-exported' and 'If QtWarningFilter moves to qtlog.py -> qutebrowser/utils/log.py:404-419 must be removed or re-exported'. Leaving both definitions in log.py after adding them to qtlog.py would create duplicate symbols and mislead callers. The exact_code_regions identify lines 362–371 (hide_qt_warning) and 404–419 (QtWarningFilter) as the removal targets.
preserved_findings: Both symbols still live exclusively in `qutebrowser/utils/log.py` (lines 362-371 and 404-419 respectively); they are absent from `qutebrowser/utils/qtlog.py` entirely
If hide_qt_warning is added to qtlog.py -> qutebrowser/utils/log.py:362-371 must be removed or re-exported
If QtWarningFilter moves to qtlog.py -> qutebrowser/utils/log.py:404-419 must be removed or re-exported
qutebrowser/utils/log.py:362-371
qutebrowser/utils/log.py:404-419

edits.change_rationale: The must_co_edit_relations state: 'If hide_qt_warning moves to qtlog.py -> qutebrowser/browser/qtnetworkdownloads.py:124 import must change'. The call_chain_context confirms: 'qutebrowser/browser/qtnetworkdownloads.py:124 -> log.hide_qt_warning() [must change to qtlog.hide_qt_warning()]'. Once hide_qt_warning is removed from log.py, this call site will fail at runtime unless updated.
preserved_findings: qutebrowser/browser/qtnetworkdownloads.py:124 -> log.hide_qt_warning() [must change to qtlog.hide_qt_warning()]
If hide_qt_warning moves to qtlog.py -> qutebrowser/browser/qtnetworkdownloads.py:124 import must change

edits.change_rationale: The must_co_edit_relations state: 'If hide_qt_warning moves to qtlog.py -> tests/unit/utils/test_log.py::TestHideQtWarning must move to test_qtlog.py'. The file currently exists (53 lines) but contains only TestQtMessageHandler; it has no coverage for hide_qt_warning or QtWarningFilter. The full TestHideQtWarning class (test_log.py:343–369) must be reproduced here, with all import references updated from `log.hide_qt_warning` to `qtlog.hide_qt_warning`. req-006 (TO_BE_PARTIAL) also requires that multi-logger consistency be verified, so the moved tests should cover the default 'qt' logger in addition to the existing 'qt-tests' fixture.
preserved_findings: If hide_qt_warning moves to qtlog.py -> tests/unit/utils/test_log.py::TestHideQtWarning must move to test_qtlog.py
tests/unit/utils/test_log.py:352 -> log.hide_qt_warning() [must change to qtlog.hide_qt_warning()]
tests/unit/utils/test_log.py:366 -> log.hide_qt_warning() [must change to qtlog.hide_qt_warning()]
test_log.py:343-369 (`TestHideQtWarning`) uses a single fixture logger `'qt-tests'` for both `test_unfiltered` (line 351) and `test_filtered` (line 365)
test_log.py:354 -- qt_logger.warning('Hello World') with pattern 'World' (not at start)
test_log.py:355-358 -- asserts 1 record captured, levelname=='WARNING', message=='Hello World'
test_log.py:361 -- parametrize value 'Hello' with comment '# exact match' exercises the exact-match suppression path
test_log.py:362 -- parametrize value 'Hello World' with comment '# match at start of line' directly exercises prefix-match suppression
test_log.py:363 -- parametrize value '  Hello World  ' (leading + trailing spaces) with comment '# match with spaces' directly exercises the whitespace-trimming path
test_log.py:369 -- assert not caplog.records confirms zero records captured (complete suppression)

edits.change_rationale: The must_co_edit_relations state: 'If hide_qt_warning moves to qtlog.py -> tests/unit/utils/test_log.py::TestHideQtWarning must move to test_qtlog.py'. The exact_code_regions identify lines 343–369 as the TestHideQtWarning class. Once the class is reproduced in test_qtlog.py and hide_qt_warning is removed from log.py, the references at lines 352 and 366 (`log.hide_qt_warning(...)`) will fail at import/runtime. The class must be removed from this file to eliminate the stale references.
preserved_findings: The test suite in `tests/unit/utils/test_log.py` (class `TestHideQtWarning`, lines 343-369) still calls `log.hide_qt_warning(...)` on lines 352 and 366, referencing the old module.
tests/unit/utils/test_log.py:352 -> log.hide_qt_warning() [must change to qtlog.hide_qt_warning()]
tests/unit/utils/test_log.py:366 -> log.hide_qt_warning() [must change to qtlog.hide_qt_warning()]
If hide_qt_warning moves to qtlog.py -> tests/unit/utils/test_log.py::TestHideQtWarning must move to test_qtlog.py
tests/unit/utils/test_log.py:343-369
